lessons.title: Answering & Wrong Numbers
lessons.scene: Your phone rings. It's an unknown number. You answer, and a stranger is asking for someone named Ramesh.

steps.prompt: Tell them there's no Ramesh here — wrong number

choices.kannada: Illi Ramesh antha yaaru illa. Wrong number.
choices.script: ಇಲ್ಲಿ ರಮೇಶ್ ಅಂತ ಯಾರೂ ಇಲ್ಲ. ರಾಂಗ್ ನಂಬರ್.
choices.pronunciation: IL-li ra-MESH an-tha YAA-ruu IL-la. raang NAM-bar
choices.english: There's no one called Ramesh here. Wrong number.

choices.hint: You're not Ramesh! Politely tell them no one by that name is here: 'Ramesh antha yaaru illa'.
That implies Ramesh is you/here. Since he isn't, say 'yaaru illa' (no one) — it's a wrong number.

correctReaction.speaker: Caller
correctReaction.kannada: Oh, sorry saar. Tappi number haakide.
correctReaction.script: ಓ, ಸಾರಿ ಸಾರ್. ತಪ್ಪಿ ನಂಬರ್ ಹಾಕಿದೆ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: oh, SAA-ri saar. TAP-pi NAM-bar HAA-ki-de
correctReaction.english: Oh, sorry sir. I dialled the wrong number.

wrongReaction.speaker: Caller
wrongReaction.english: Ramesh, I'm calling from the bank...

steps.grammarTip: 'Antha' (or 'anta') marks names and quotes — 'Ramesh antha yaaru illa' = 'no one called Ramesh'. 'Idaara?' is the polite 'is he/she there?' On the phone, people answer with the English 'hello'.

steps.speaker: You
steps.english: No problem, it happens. I'll hang up.

lessons.title: Giving Directions to Your Home
lessons.scene: A delivery agent calls — he's near your area in HSR Layout but can't find your apartment. He needs you to guide him to the gate over the phone.

steps.speaker: Delivery Agent
steps.kannada: Saar, naanu delivery. Nimma area-ge bandidini, address sigtilla.
steps.script: ಸಾರ್, ನಾನು ಡೆಲಿವರಿ. ನಿಮ್ಮ ಏರಿಯಾಗೆ ಬಂದಿದೀನಿ, ಅಡ್ರೆಸ್ ಸಿಗ್ತಿಲ್ಲ.
steps.pronunciation: saar, NAA-nu de-LI-va-ri. NIM-ma EH-ri-yaa-ge ban-di-DEE-ni, AD-res SIG-til-la
steps.english: Sir, I'm the delivery (agent). I've come to your area but can't find the address.

steps.prompt: Ask him where exactly he is right now

choices.hint: You know who he is. To locate him, ask WHERE: 'elli iddira?' (where are you?).
'Yaavaga' (when) won't help you guide him. Ask 'elli iddira?' — where is he now.

correctReaction.speaker: Delivery Agent
correctReaction.kannada: Naanu medical shop hattira iddini saar.
correctReaction.script: ನಾನು ಮೆಡಿಕಲ್ ಶಾಪ್ ಹತ್ತಿರ ಇದ್ದೀನಿ ಸಾರ್.
correctReaction.pronunciation: NAA-nu ME-di-kal shaap HAT-ti-ra id-DEE-ni saar
correctReaction.english: I'm near the medical (pharmacy) shop, sir.

wrongReaction.speaker: Delivery Agent
wrongReaction.kannada: Saar, naanu delivery agent antha helde alva!
wrongReaction.script: ಸಾರ್, ನಾನು ಡೆಲಿವರಿ ಏಜೆಂಟ್ ಅಂತ ಹೇಳ್ದೆ ಅಲ್ವಾ!
wrongReaction.pronunciation: saar, NAA-nu de-LI-va-ri agent an-tha HEL-de al-va
wrongReaction.english: Sir, I told you I'm the delivery agent, right!

vocab.english: (I) can't find it / it isn't found

steps.grammarTip: 'Iddira?' is the polite 'are you?' for asking someone's state or location; pair it with 'elli' (where): 'elli iddira?'. Note 'sigtilla' = 'isn't found / I can't find it' — a super common way to say something's missing.

steps.prompt: Guide him: from the medical shop, go straight and take the second right

choices.kannada: Alli inda nera banni, eradane balagade tirugi.
choices.script: ಅಲ್ಲಿಂದ ನೇರ ಬನ್ನಿ, ಎರಡನೇ ಬಲಗಡೆ ತಿರುಗಿ.
choices.pronunciation: AL-lin-da NE-ra BAN-ni, e-RA-da-ne BA-la-ga-de ti-RU-gi
choices.english: From there come straight, take the second right.

choices.hint: Fine in a pinch, but you were asked to guide him in. Direct him: 'nera banni, eradane balagade tirugi'.

choices.english: Go straight, take the first left.
choices.hint: Wrong turn — that's the FIRST LEFT. You wanted the SECOND RIGHT: 'eradane balagade'.

wrongReaction.speaker: Delivery Agent
wrongReaction.english: Left or right sir, tell me correctly.

steps.grammarTip: '-inda' = 'from': 'alli-inda' (from there), 'mane-inda' (from home). Ordinals add '-ane': 'eradane' (second), 'mooru-ne' (third). Chain them for directions: 'eradane balagade' = the second right.

steps.prompt: Finish: it's the blue building, our gate is on the left. Wait there.

choices.kannada: Adu blue building, namma gate edagade ide. Alli nilli.
choices.script: ಅದು ಬ್ಲೂ ಬಿಲ್ಡಿಂಗ್, ನಮ್ಮ ಗೇಟ್ ಎಡಗಡೆ ಇದೆ. ಅಲ್ಲಿ ನಿಲ್ಲಿ.
choices.pronunciation: a-du bloo BIL-ding, NAM-ma gate E-da-ga-de i-de. AL-li NIL-li
choices.english: It's the blue building, our gate is on the left. Wait there.

choices.kannada: Adu blue building, gate beda. Hogi.
choices.script: ಅದು ಬ್ಲೂ ಬಿಲ್ಡಿಂಗ್, ಗೇಟ್ ಬೇಡ. ಹೋಗಿ.
choices.pronunciation: a-du bloo BIL-ding, gate BEH-da. HO-gi
choices.english: It's the blue building, no gate. Go away.
choices.hint: 'Gate beda' and 'hogi' (go) send him off! Tell him where the gate is and to wait: 'gate edagade ide, alli nilli'.

choices.kannada: Namma gate balagade ide, bega hogi.
choices.script: ನಮ್ಮ ಗೇಟ್ ಬಲಗಡೆ ಇದೆ, ಬೇಗ ಹೋಗಿ.
choices.pronunciation: NAM-ma gate BA-la-ga-de i-de, BEH-ga HO-gi
choices.english: Our gate is on the right, go quickly.
choices.hint: Wrong side, and 'hogi' tells him to leave. Say the gate is on the left and to wait: 'edagade ide, alli nilli'.

correctReaction.speaker: Delivery Agent
correctReaction.kannada: Sigtu saar! Blue building, gate kanstide. Bartini.
correctReaction.script: ಸಿಕ್ತು ಸಾರ್! ಬ್ಲೂ ಬಿಲ್ಡಿಂಗ್, ಗೇಟ್ ಕಾಣ್ಸ್ತಿದೆ. ಬರ್ತೀನಿ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: SIK-tu saar! bloo BIL-ding, gate KAANS-ti-de. BAR-tee-ni
correctReaction.english: Found it sir! Blue building, I can see the gate. I'm coming.

wrongReaction.speaker: Delivery Agent
wrongReaction.kannada: Saar, gate elli antha sariyaagi heli please.
wrongReaction.script: ಸಾರ್, ಗೇಟ್ ಎಲ್ಲಿ ಅಂತ ಸರಿಯಾಗಿ ಹೇಳಿ ಪ್ಲೀಸ್.
wrongReaction.pronunciation: saar, gate EL-li an-tha sa-ri-YAA-gi HEH-li pleez
wrongReaction.english: Sir, please tell me properly where the gate is.

steps.grammarTip: Possessives: 'namma' (our), 'nanna' (my), 'nimma' (your). 'Illi' = here, 'alli' = there — 'alli nilli' tells someone to wait at a far point. And 'sigtu!' (found it!) is the happy phrase you'll hear when directions finally land.
